title: Hot and Sour Soup

seo.title: Authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe (Easy & Delicious!)

seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Hot and Sour Soup, bursting with authentic Chinese flavors.

description: Hot and Sour Soup is a classic Chinese appetizer, renowned for its intriguing balance of piquant spice and tangy vinegar. This comforting and robust soup features a medley of ingredients like tofu, mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and often pork, all brought together in a savory broth thickened to perfection. It is a beloved dish that awakens the palate with every spoonful, offering a delightful warmth and complex flavor profile.

ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions.title: Prepare Dried Ingredients
instructions.text: Soak dried shiitake and wood ear mushrooms in hot water for 20-30 minutes until softened. Drain, squeeze out excess water, remove stems from shiitake, and thinly slice both types of mushrooms.

instructions.title: Prepare Meat and Tofu
instructions.text: Thinly slice pork tenderloin into matchsticks. Drain firm tofu and cut into small cubes. Slice bamboo shoots into thin strips.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Pork
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a tablespoon of cooking oil over medium-high heat. Add the sliced pork and stir-fry until cooked through, about 3-4 minutes. Remove pork and set aside.

instructions.title: Simmer the Broth
instructions.text: Pour chicken or vegetable broth into the pot. Add sliced shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Season the Soup
instructions.text: Return the cooked pork to the pot. Add soy sauce, rice vinegar, and white pepper. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. The soup should have a good balance of hot, sour, and savory.

instructions.title: Thicken and Finish
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk cornstarch with 3 tablespoons of cold water to create a slurry. Slowly add the slurry to the simmering soup, stirring constantly until it thickens to your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Add Egg and Serve
instructions.text: While stirring the soup in a circular motion, slowly drizzle in the beaten egg, creating delicate egg ribbons. Remove from heat immediately. Stir in sesame oil. Ladle into bowls, garnish with chopped green onions, and a drizzle of chili oil if desired.

tips: For the best texture, always add the cornstarch slurry slowly to the simmering soup while stirring constantly to avoid lumps and achieve a smooth, silky consistency.
To intensify the sourness, use good quality Chinkiang vinegar (black vinegar) in addition to or instead of plain rice vinegar. For a vegetarian version, omit pork and use vegetable broth.
Hot and Sour Soup is best served immediately. If storing leftovers, keep them refrigerated for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of broth if it has thickened too much.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of Chinese cuisine with one of its most celebrated creations: Hot and Sour Soup. This iconic dish, a staple on menus worldwide, is a symphony of flavors that tantalizes the taste buds with its unique blend of piquant spice and tangy sourness. More than just a starter, Hot and Sour Soup is a comforting, complex, and incredibly satisfying experience, perfect for warming you from the inside out. Whether you’re a seasoned chef or a curious home cook, mastering this classic is a rewarding endeavor. Prepare to dive into the rich history, essential ingredients, and step-by-step techniques to create an authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe that will impress even the most discerning palates.

## What is Hot and Sour Soup?

Hot and Sour Soup, or Suan La Tang in Mandarin, is a traditional Chinese soup renowned for its distinctive flavor profile. As its name suggests, it expertly balances a fiery kick from white pepper and chili oil with a tangy sourness primarily derived from rice vinegar. Beyond these dominant flavors, the soup boasts a rich umami depth from ingredients like soy sauce and often a touch of sesame oil. Texturally, it's a marvel, typically thickened with cornstarch slurry to achieve a velvety, slightly viscous consistency, studded with an array of finely sliced ingredients. Common additions include slivers of pork or chicken, rehydrated dried shiitake mushrooms, bamboo shoots, tofu, and wisps of egg flower, creating a delightful interplay of chewiness and tenderness in every spoonful. Originating from Sichuan province, it has since become popular across various regions of China and has been adapted globally.

## Ingredients You'll Need

Creating an authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe starts with selecting quality ingredients. Here's what you'll typically need to gather:

For the Broth Base:
*   6 cups chicken or vegetable broth (low sodium preferred)
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce (light or regular)
*   1 tablespoon dark soy sauce (for color and deeper flavor)
*   1/4 cup rice vinegar (or black vinegar for a more complex tang)
*   1-2 tablespoons chili oil (adjust to your spice preference)
*   1 teaspoon white pepper (crucial for the "hot" element)
*   1/2 teaspoon sugar (to balance flavors)
*   1/4 teaspoon sesame oil (for aromatic finish)

For the Fillings:
*   4 oz lean pork loin or chicken breast, thinly sliced into matchsticks
*   4-6 dried shiitake mushrooms, rehydrated and thinly sliced (reserve mushroom liquid)
*   1 (8 oz) can bamboo shoots, drained and thinly sliced into matchsticks
*   1 (8 oz) block firm or extra-firm tofu, pressed and cut into small matchsticks
*   1/2 cup wood ear mushrooms (fresh or rehydrated), thinly sliced (optional)

For Thickening and Finishing:
*   2 large eggs, whisked
*   3 tablespoons cornstarch mixed with 4 tablespoons cold water (cornstarch slurry)
*   2 green onions, sliced (for garnish)
*   Optional: Fresh cilantro, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to prepare your perfect Hot and Sour Soup Recipe:

1.  **Prepare Ingredients:** Slice all your proteins and vegetables as described. Rehydrate dried mushrooms by soaking them in hot water for 20-30 minutes, then squeeze dry, remove stems, and slice. Reserve the mushroom liquid if desired, straining it before adding to the broth for extra flavor. Whisk the eggs in a small bowl and prepare the cornstarch slurry.

2.  **Simmer the Broth:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, combine the chicken or vegetable broth, soy sauce, dark soy sauce, and any reserved mushroom liquid. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium heat.

3.  **Add Proteins and Vegetables:** Add the sliced pork or chicken, rehydrated shiitake mushrooms, bamboo shoots, tofu, and wood ear mushrooms (if using) to the simmering broth. Cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the meat is cooked through and the vegetables are tender-crisp.

4.  **Season the Soup:** Stir in the rice vinegar, chili oil, white pepper, and sugar. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed. This is the stage where you fine-tune the "hot" and "sour" balance. Don't be shy to add more vinegar or white pepper to your liking.

5.  **Thicken the Soup:** Give your cornstarch slurry a quick stir to ensure it's well mixed. Slowly pour the slurry into the simmering soup in a thin stream, stirring constantly to prevent lumps. Continue stirring until the soup thickens to your desired consistency. It should be noticeably thicker but still pourable.

6.  **Add Egg Flower:** Once the soup is thickened and simmering gently, slowly drizzle the whisked eggs into the soup in a thin stream while stirring continuously with a fork or chopsticks. This creates delicate strands of "egg flower."

7.  **Finish and Serve:** Remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the sesame oil. Ladle the Hot and Sour Soup into bowls and garnish generously with sliced green onions and fresh cilantro, if desired. Serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Hot and Sour Soup

*   **Quality Ingredients Matter:** High-quality broth forms the foundation. Don't skimp on good soy sauce, fresh vinegar, and aromatic sesame oil.
*   **Balance is Key:** The beauty of Hot and Sour Soup lies in its balance. Continuously taste and adjust the levels of sourness (vinegar), spiciness (white pepper, chili oil), and umami (soy sauce, broth) to suit your preference.
*   **Don't Over-Thicken:** Add the cornstarch slurry gradually. You can always add more, but it's hard to thin it out once it's too thick. The goal is a velvety, not gloopy, texture.
*   **The "Hot" is White Pepper:** While chili oil adds heat, the distinctive "hot" element of this soup traditionally comes from white pepper. Don't substitute it with black pepper for an authentic flavor.
*   **Black Vinegar for Depth:** While rice vinegar works well, Chinese black vinegar (Chinkiang vinegar) offers a deeper, more complex tang that elevates the Hot and Sour Soup Recipe to another level.
*   **Egg Flower Technique:** Pour the eggs slowly from a height while stirring the soup in a circular motion. This creates delicate, feathery strands rather than large clumps.

## Serving Suggestions

Hot and Sour Soup is a versatile dish that can be enjoyed in many ways. It makes an excellent appetizer for any Chinese meal, preceding dishes like General Tso's Chicken, Kung Pao Shrimp, or Beef with Broccoli. It's also hearty enough to be a light meal on its own, especially when paired with a side of steamed rice or a crispy spring roll. For a more substantial lunch, serve it alongside potstickers or a simple stir-fried noodle dish. A sprinkle of extra chili oil or a dash of black vinegar at the table allows diners to customize their bowl to perfection.

## Variations and Substitutions

This classic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe is highly adaptable, allowing for delicious variations:

*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** Omit the pork/chicken and use vegetable broth. Ensure your soy sauce is vegan-friendly (most are). You can add more tofu, extra mushrooms, or other vegetables like carrots, cabbage, or water chestnuts.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Use tamari instead of soy sauce and ensure your broth and chili oil are gluten-free.
*   **Different Proteins:** Instead of pork or chicken, try shrimp, shredded duck, or even thinly sliced beef. Add shrimp towards the end as they cook quickly.
*   **Mushroom Medley:** Experiment with various types of mushrooms like enoki, king oyster, or crimini for added texture and earthy flavor.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of white pepper and chili oil to your heat tolerance. For extra kick, consider adding a pinch of dried red chili flakes.
*   **Sweetness:** A tiny pinch more sugar can mellow the sourness and spiciness if you find it too intense.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Why is my Hot and Sour Soup not sour enough?**
A: The sourness primarily comes from vinegar. Add more rice vinegar or Chinese black vinegar, a tablespoon at a time, until it reaches your desired tang.

**Q: My soup isn't hot enough, even with chili oil. What's wrong?**
A: The "hot" in Hot and Sour Soup largely comes from white pepper, not just chili oil. Ensure you're using fresh white pepper. You can add more to taste or a pinch of dried red chili flakes for an extra kick.

**Q: How do I prevent my cornstarch slurry from clumping?**
A: Always mix cornstarch with cold water before adding it to hot liquid. Pour it slowly into the simmering soup while continuously stirring. If clumps form, you can try straining the soup, but it's best to prevent them.

**Q: Can I make Hot and Sour Soup ahead of time?**
A: While best served fresh, you can prepare the base of the soup a day in advance. Add the cornstarch slurry and eggs just before serving, as reheating can sometimes affect the texture of the thickened soup and egg strands.

**Q: What kind of tofu should I use?**
A: Firm or extra-firm tofu holds its shape best in the soup. Silken tofu is too delicate and will likely fall apart.

Mastering a classic like Hot and Sour Soup is a testament to the joy of cooking and the richness of Chinese culinary traditions. This comprehensive Hot and Sour Soup Recipe provides all the tools you need to create a bowl that perfectly balances the iconic hot, sour, and savory notes. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to delight in a truly authentic and incredibly satisfying soup. Enjoy the warmth and complexity of this beloved Chinese staple right in your own kitchen!
